title: Tool Use and Agentic Workflows
snippet:
@@ -26,4 @@ (25 before, 28 after)

Tool use — also referred to as function calling — enables a language model to emit structured tool call content blocks within a response, signalling that a caller-defined function should be invoked with specified arguments. The caller executes the function and returns results in a subsequent mes...

title: Open question: What introspection limitations exist for @beta_tool schema generation, and how are edge cases handled?
snippet:
@@ -23,4 @@ (22 before, 21 after)

## The Gap

The knowledge base asserts that `@beta_tool` derives a JSON schema from a function's type annotations and docstring, but provides no information about what happens in edge cases or unsupported scenarios. Specifically, the following questions are unanswered:

title: JSON Schema Generation from Python Functions
snippet:
@@ -22,4 @@ (21 before, 1 after)

The `@beta_tool` decorator applies static introspection to a Python function, extracting argument names, type annotations, and the docstring to produce a structured JSON schema conforming to the tool definition format expected by the Anthropic API. The generated schema includes a `name`, `descri...

title: Server-Sent Events (SSE)
snippet:
@@ -21,4 @@ (20 before, 1 after)

Server-Sent Events is the transport mechanism underlying streaming responses in the Anthropic API. When a caller requests a streaming response, the server holds the HTTP connection open and emits a sequence of SSE frames, each carrying a typed event and associated JSON payload, until the respons...

title: Tool Use (Function Calling)
snippet:
@@ -22,4 @@ (21 before, 1 after)

Tool use enables the model to emit tool call content blocks within a response, signalling that a specific named function should be invoked with provided arguments. The caller is responsible for executing the function and returning results in a subsequent message. The Anthropic Python SDK automat...

title: Error Handling and Resilience Mechanisms
snippet:
@@ -26,4 @@ (25 before, 32 after)

Production API integrations must handle transient network failures, rate limiting, and server-side errors without requiring callers to implement bespoke recovery logic. The Anthropic Python SDK addresses this through three complementary mechanisms: a structured exception hierarchy that allows er...

title: Asynchronous API Client
snippet:
@@ -22,4 @@ (21 before, 1 after)

The `AsyncAnthropic` class is the asynchronous client provided by the Anthropic Python SDK, powered by `httpx`. All API calls must be awaited inside an `async` function, enabling concurrent execution within an event loop. An optional `aiohttp` backend can be substituted via the `anthropic[aiohtt...

title: Message Batches API
snippet:
@@ -21,4 @@ (20 before, 1 after)

The Message Batches API is accessible under `client.messages.batches` and accepts the same parameters as the standard Messages API, with each request identified by a caller-supplied `custom_id`. Batch creation returns a `MessageBatch` object; callers poll the batch's `processing_status` field an...

title: Request Timeout
snippet:
@@ -21,4 @@ (20 before, 1 after)

The Anthropic Python SDK sets a default request timeout of 10 minutes. For non-streaming requests that are projected to exceed this threshold, the SDK raises a `ValueError` as a pre-flight guard. Streaming requests and requests with explicit timeout overrides bypass this guard. The timeout value...

title: Synchronous API Client
snippet:
@@ -21,4 @@ (20 before, 1 after)

The `Anthropic` class is the synchronous client provided by the Anthropic Python SDK. It exposes methods such as `client.messages.create()`, `client.messages.count_tokens()`, and `client.messages.batches.*` that block the calling thread until a response is received. It is powered by `httpx` and ...

title: Automatic Retry with Exponential Backoff
snippet:
@@ -21,4 @@ (20 before, 1 after)

The Anthropic Python SDK applies automatic retries with exponential backoff to connection errors and HTTP responses with status codes 408, 409, 429, and any code ≥ 500. The default retry count is 2 and is configurable via the `max_retries` parameter on the client constructor or per-request via `...

title: Pagination
snippet:
@@ -21,4 @@ (20 before, 1 after)

List endpoints in the Anthropic Python SDK return auto-paginating iterators that transparently fetch subsequent pages as the caller iterates. Manual pagination control is available via `.has_next_page()`, `.next_page_info()`, `.get_next_page()`, and `.last_id` methods on the response object.

Pa...

title: Error Hierarchy
snippet:
@@ -21,4 @@ (20 before, 1 after)

The Anthropic Python SDK organises all API-related exceptions under `anthropic.APIError` as the root base class. Specific subtypes include `APIConnectionError` for network-level failures, `RateLimitError` for HTTP 429 responses, and `APIStatusError` for other HTTP error codes. The SDK also expor...

title: Streaming Response
snippet:
@@ -21,4 @@ (20 before, 1 after)

Streaming responses are delivered via Server-Sent Events (SSE), allowing callers to process output tokens as they arrive. The Anthropic Python SDK exposes two consumption paths: `client.messages.create(..., stream=True)`, which returns a low-memory iterable of raw stream events without accumulat...
